slides.title: Changes in Use Cases and Requirements Document

polls.text: Is the WG happy to work on the architecture and protocol documents in parallel?

chat.author: Rohan Mahy
chat.text: welcome to DIEM at IETF 126!

chat.author: Daniel Gillmor
chat.text: yep i heard him loud and clear

chat.author: Stuart Card
chat.text: heard him

chat.author: Rahel Fainchtein
chat.text: Heard him

chat.author: Rohan Mahy
chat.text:
Note: the individual documents which have made comments about the architecture are listed in the Agenda here:
https://datatracker.ietf.org/meeting/126/materials/agenda-126-diem-02

chat.author: Felix Linker
chat.text: Clarifying question to chairs: What do you mean by "now" in the second question?

chat.author: Felix Linker
chat.text: Now == in this meeting?

chat.author: Rohan Mahy
chat.text: That we don't need to wait for some other decision for it to be relevant

chat.author: Felix Linker
chat.text: Let's define "this order" as "<=" :)

chat.author: Sarah Jennings
chat.text: Assuming we work in parallel, the now is meaningless in the question

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: ^ agreed

chat.author: Daniel Gillmor
chat.text: seems clear to me that trying to nail down a protocol concretely will help make the architecture document more plausible.

chat.author: Alex Rosenberg
chat.text: @Daniel +1 -- I find thinking about the protocol helps

chat.author: Peter Koch
chat.text: You don't know whether you are at the apex

chat.author: Sarah Jennings
chat.text: Being called Jim is not a prerequisite for having an opinion on this slide

chat.author: Nic Williams
chat.text: The IETF collects powerful DNS-minded Jims

chat.author: Allison Mankin
chat.text: It looks like Rohan's slides are not linked to the agenda?

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: Phew, glad you said that, was feeling left out

chat.author: Peter Koch
chat.text: fallback to other RR types is generally a bad idea (cf. MX vs. fallback to A which lead to the "Null MX" construct)

chat.author: Daniel Gillmor
chat.text: wow, dig -t txt mit.edu  is indeed truncated when accessed over UDP

chat.author: Sarah Jennings
chat.text: Allison - were removed and re-added - we'll make sure they're linked afterwards

chat.author: Allison Mankin
chat.text: I hoped to

chat.author: Allison Mankin
chat.text: look ahead, why I asked

chat.author: Sarah Jennings
chat.text: Uploaded, take a look now in materials?

chat.author: Jim Reid
chat.text: The WG needs more Jims. rpp and regext WGs have a few. We should beat them. :grinning:

chat.author: Peter Koch
chat.text: 'versioned' RR types aren't successful either. It makes complying wth RFC3597 RR tytpe transparency close to impossible

chat.author: Felix Linker
chat.text:
Sarah Jennings said:

Uploaded, take a look now in materials?

they're there

chat.author: James Mozley
chat.text: Nic is going to change his name to Jim, it's still three letters

chat.author: Allison Mankin
chat.text: Thanks Sarah

chat.author: Sarah Jennings
chat.text: Peter, please do feel free to mention your comments at the mic - they may not get noted in the minutes otherwise

chat.author: Jim Reid
chat.text: peter's not in the room...

chat.author: Peter Koch
chat.text: i'm not in the room, maybe the minute taker can copy them?

chat.author: Dan York
chat.text: DANE relies on DNSSEC

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: Peter, I can relay on the mic if you wish?

chat.author: Peter Koch
chat.text: thanks, Tommy!

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: my pleasure!

chat.author: James Mozley
chat.text: @Tommy happy to be involved in a discussion on record types/usage in the DIEM context if you need a body

chat.author: Sarah Jennings
chat.text: Thanks Tommy! Sorry Peter, wasn't sure if you were remote or not

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: Brilliant, I'll be sure to include you Jim

chat.author: Peter Koch
chat.text: np

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: no, the other Jim

chat.author: Jim Reid
chat.text: I've asked the note taker to capture peter's comments.

chat.author: Suzanne Woolf
chat.text: Tommy I think they're all "the other Jim".....

chat.author: Jim Reid
chat.text: Peter can also post them to the list. :grinning:

chat.author: Peter Koch
chat.text: thanks, Jim.  It's kinda alarming we're now continuously on the same page ;-)

chat.author: Jim Reid
chat.text: We need to stop doing that Peter.

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: @Suzanne that's equitable, I'm supportive

chat.author: Peter Koch
chat.text: I agree (oh no, wait ...)

chat.author: Rahel Fainchtein
chat.text: Thanks

chat.author: Sarah Jennings
chat.text: The laughter was in response to something in the room!

chat.author: Daniel Gillmor
chat.text: new RRTypes are cheap

chat.author: Peter Koch
chat.text: "choice" of DNSSEC should probably take into account the adversary effect of forged responses - one way or another

chat.author: Rohan Mahy
chat.text: someone's AI assistant started talking in the room

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: indeed, sorry for any misunderstanding, my "so do we all" was in response to someone's phone saying "I understand that you want to learn more about encrypted clienthello..."

chat.author: Allison Mankin
chat.text: Hahaha

chat.author: Laura Scalone
chat.text: did anyone talk about the problem related with the discovery of DIEM names, the possibility of a global registry and what that might entail?

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: @Daniel technically true, but unless defining a flexible structure for a single RR type is problematic, I think it's worth it given the points are plentiful but the re-hashing of "but what will middleboxes do" is non trivial

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: but not a hill I'd die on or anything

chat.author: Dan York
chat.text: I'm going to disagree with Jim

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: @Laura: to my knowledge, I think everyone is operating on the presumption that the name is known in advance

chat.author: Dan York
chat.text: I'm not in the room... so can't come to the mic

chat.author: Felix Linker
chat.text: Agreed, Rahel, one can implement authorization using DNSSEC, but that such a mechanism would be an extension of current DNSSEC as well. Which is my point. The chain of authorization that DNSSEC gives you is not the one required by some use cases.

chat.author: Nic Williams
chat.text: @laura Jim (on mic) wrote a companion draft that covers the pro/con of it

chat.author: Allison Mankin
chat.text: Can you just speak remotely, Dan York?

chat.author: Peter Koch
chat.text: +1 Dan (and here we go again); but i tend to agree that the discussion of the pros and cons and feasability is probably not the best use of the meeting time right now and here

chat.author: James Mozley
chat.text: In practice aren't most people using a service provider for DNS? There will either have the geek  tools to support DNS such as "sign my zone" or not (in which case change provider!). So the user does not need to be a DNS geek I think, but know that DNSSEC signing is a thing they should do.

chat.author: Sarah Jennings
chat.text: If you can't speak Dan, you can also put in chat and someone can convey on mic

chat.author: Dan York
chat.text: I can come down in 10 minutes

chat.author: Laura Scalone
chat.text: @Nic I did read the draft dnsarch and the section that talks about that only has 1 paragraph. that's why I was asking

chat.author: Dan York
chat.text: But you all will have moved on :-)

chat.author: Daniel Gillmor
chat.text: Dan can't speak remotely because he'll interrupt the room he's in

chat.author: Nic Williams
chat.text: @Laura, gotcha. I think it's a good topic for discussion it's not been brought up yet so far AFAIK

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: @dkg That sounds like a problem for the other room /s

chat.author: Allison Mankin
chat.text: Ah. Note I wrote Dan York because we have multi-Dan as well as multi-Jim

chat.author: Dan York
chat.text: On my way down now

chat.author: Felix Linker
chat.text: @Daniel Gillmor I think Rahel's point was that one can build a general purpose authorization mechanism on top off DNSSEC.

chat.author: Daniel Gillmor
chat.text: it just seems like we're not going to have this just be DNSSEC -- there are other things that have to be in the mix, so let's not kid ourselves that DNSSEC solves the whole thing.

chat.author: Felix Linker
chat.text:
Daniel Gillmor said:

it just seems like we're not going to have this just be DNSSEC -- there are other things that have to be in the mix, so let's not kid ourselves that DNSSEC solves the whole thing.

agreed

chat.author: Daniel Gillmor
chat.text: (though it would if we were just talking about marking ICRC things with a record in the ICRC.example zone)

chat.author: Sarah Jennings
chat.text: This also feels like something where getting into protocol design for an emblem type alongside architecture might make this clearer

chat.author: Jim Reid
chat.text: +1 dkg DNSSEC can't be the magic bullet that solves everything

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: +1 Sarah, likely to be helpful

chat.author: Allison Mankin
chat.text: @dkg, definitely not saying DNSSEC does it all, but DNSSEC is a utility

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: Some people, when confronted with a problem, think “I know, I’ll use ~regular expressions.~ DNSSEC” Now they have two problems.

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: sadly my markdown skills are not good enough for that joke to be funny.

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: I thought it was meta joke about using markdown then having another problem

chat.author: Felix Linker
chat.text:
Orie Steele said:

sadly my markdown skills are not good enough for that joke to be funny.

what a shame :zip_it:

chat.author: Felix Linker
chat.text: (try two ~)

chat.author: Sarah Jennings
chat.text: Can someone capture Laura's comments while she's speaking?

chat.author: Sarah Jennings
chat.text: For notes

chat.author: Felix Linker
chat.text: I think 40% is quite good actually

chat.author: Felix Linker
chat.text: Thought it would be less

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: you had to ask right as I cmd-tabbed for something, sorry Sarah and Laura. Thank you for taking notes!

chat.author: Alex Rosenberg
chat.text: I don't think these stats matter to DIEM -- this is about validation of emblems, not random DNS queries. There's considerably more value  for DIEM.

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: We should make sure whatever we come up with has some PQ support, or agility to PQ algorithms.

chat.author: Alex Rosenberg
chat.text: @Orie +1

chat.author: Gavin Brown
chat.text: https://stats.labs.apnic.net/dnssec

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: oh right, PQ is something I tend to think of as a given, but let's do write it down / sayit out loud

chat.author: Dan York
chat.text: Here's some metrics: https://pulse.internetsociety.org/en/technologies/#metric-dnssec

chat.author: Felix Linker
chat.text:
Gavin Brown said:

https://stats.labs.apnic.net/dnssec

north korea way ahead of south korea!

chat.author: Alex Rosenberg
chat.text: @Tommy +1

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: I'll inform the Secretariat of my name upgrade

chat.author: Nic Williams
chat.text: Tommy "Jim" Jensen

chat.author: Alex Rosenberg
chat.text: In practice some emblem types, being more numerous/dynamic/coming from another database will be done with a zone delegation just to move DNS delivery to another server

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: oh, so we _are_ talking about discovery of asset identifiers separate from discovery of an asset's emblems? That wasn't clear to me

chat.author: Jim Reid
chat.text: @Nic, can I be Tommy (jim) reid?

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: Don't worry Orie, I also can't use markdown

chat.author: Nic Williams
chat.text: notionally I think discovery would include both use cases (direct to emblem, or identifier to point)

chat.author: Allison Mankin
chat.text: Tommy, I agree with NIC on both kinds of discovery

chat.author: Felix Linker
chat.text: "can I pee in this pond?" ==> dig pond.net

chat.author: Rahel Fainchtein
chat.text: Actually, yes

chat.author: Rahel Fainchtein
chat.text: I don't think it's crazy

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: Jim, mi nombre es su nombre

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: "diplomacy"

chat.author: Allison Mankin
chat.text: @Felix Linker heh, yes

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: Tommy (Jim) Reid is expressing his love for liaison statements is what I'm gathering from this. IAB slots opening up for nomination, just sayin'

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: Autonomous System = AI Agent .... amitrite?

chat.author: Jim Reid
chat.text: Love is not the word I would use for Liaison Statements. Disgust or despair would be closer. And it wouldn't necessarily be LS that's used to approve a delegation in diem.arpa.

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: Orie weaponizing AI BoF memories like a pro

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: lol

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: These are good! (per emblem type requirements)

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: Same, never thought of removability as optional, but I guess it could be

chat.author: Alex Rosenberg
chat.text: Thankfully nobody is talking about blockchain ledgers here, so removability is a great feature.

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: LOL, Alex don't jinx us I beg you

chat.author: Felix Linker
chat.text:
Tommy Jensen said:

Same, never thought of removability as optional, but I guess it could be

I guess as a requirement, it's optional for some use cases. But in the DNS, it comes so cheap, why not grab it.

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: what if the blockchain is the asset... thats why I did not like the previous slide

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: __oh god Orie why__

chat.author: Felix Linker
chat.text:
Felix Linker said:

Tommy Jensen said:

Same, never thought of removability as optional, but I guess it could be

I guess as a requirement, it's optional for some use cases. But in the DNS, it comes so cheap, why not grab it.

Or: Haven't heard of NON-removability as a requirement.

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: @Felix makes me wanna write an immutable zone draft, be careful

chat.author: Alex Rosenberg
chat.text: It can always be worse -- one use case I keep uncontrollably thinking about is "what if a DNS server delivered it's own SNMP-like data via an emblem?"

chat.author: Alex Rosenberg
chat.text: I will not propose ASN.1 for anything in the protocol, I promise.

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: It costs $0.00 to not write that comment, Alex. But here we are. <snark>

chat.author: Nic Williams
chat.text: And here I thought Alex was going to support bringing back the chaos class

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text:
I will not propose ASN.1 for anything in the protocol, I promise.

For the notes!

chat.author: Felix Linker
chat.text: That was a useful discussion, thanks for facilitating it, Rohan and Sarah!

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: Nic that square on my bingo card got checked in an AI BoF already, it's been a fun week

chat.author: Alex Rosenberg
chat.text: (Without checking, I'm assuming CBOR isn't using ASN.1 inside it.)

chat.author: Felix Linker
chat.text:
Alex Rosenberg said:

(Without checking, I'm assuming CBOR isn't using ASN.1 inside it.)

it is not

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: using AI... any timeline is achievable

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: (I'm not an AD in this room, unrelated note)

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: @Orie vibemblem

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: viblem

chat.author: Jim Reid
chat.text: You can be that AD if you want Tommy. Or is it Jim?

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: it's Charles, that way gestures vaguely and runs off
